title: Strike Timeline & Latest Updates
description: Key events and current developments in the Air Canada flight attendant labor dispute and government intervention.

sectionsDescription.events: Chronological sequence of major developments in the labor dispute
sectionsDescription.updates: Current information about government intervention and recovery process

negotiations.title: Failed Negotiations
negotiations.description: Breakdown in talks between Air Canada and CUPE over wage increases and working conditions. Union rejects company's final offer.

strikeStart.title: Strike Begins
strikeStart.description: Air Canada flight attendants officially begin strike action. All flights cancelled, affecting thousands of passengers.

govIntervention.title: Government Intervention
govIntervention.description: Jobs Minister Patty Hajdu orders binding arbitration under Section 107 of the Canada Labour Code, effectively ending the strike.

recovery.title: Recovery Period
recovery.description: Air Canada operations resume gradually. Full network restoration expected within 4-5 days of government order.

bindingArbitration.title: Binding Arbitration Process Initiated
bindingArbitration.description: The Canada Industrial Relations Board will oversee fast-tracked arbitration. Both parties must accept the arbitrator's final decision on wages, benefits, and working conditions.

operationalRecovery.title: 4-5 Day Recovery Timeline
operationalRecovery.description: Air Canada expects gradual restoration of flight operations over 4-5 days. Passengers should expect continued delays and cancellations during this recovery period.

unionResponse.title: CUPE Statement on Forced Arbitration
unionResponse.description: Union leadership expresses disappointment with government intervention but confirms compliance with back-to-work order while arbitration proceeds.
